title: Notarial Activities
description: Description of notarial activities performed by the notary in accordance with the Notarial Law Act.
content:
A notary, as a public official, performs notarial activities in accordance with applicable law, in particular the Notarial Law Act. Notarial activities are performed at the notarial office or, in special circumstances, outside it (e.g. when a person participating in a notarial activity cannot appear in person at the office due to health reasons).

Pursuant to Art. 79 of the Act of 14 February 1991 — Notarial Law, a notary performs the following activities:

  • 1) draws up notarial deeds;
  • 1a) draws up certificates of inheritance;
  • 1b) takes actions regarding the European Certificate of Succession;
  • 2) draws up attestations and certifications;
  • 3) serves declarations;
  • 4) drafts minutes and protocols;
  • 5) draws up protests of bills of exchange and cheques;
  • 6) accepts for safekeeping money, securities, documents, and data on IT data carriers referred to in the provisions on the computerisation of public entities;
  • 7) draws up certified copies, copies, and excerpts of documents;
  • 8) draws up, at the request of the parties, drafts of deeds, declarations, and other documents;
  • 8a) files applications for entry in the land and mortgage register together with the documents constituting the basis for such entry;
  • 9) performs other activities provided for under separate regulations.
metaTitle: Notarial Activities | Notarial Office Aleksandra Durys
metaDescription: Notarial activities performed by the notary in Garwolin: notarial deeds, certificates of inheritance, minutes, safekeeping of documents, and more.
